pattern: A stack collapses adjacent equal pairs in one pass: push each character, but pop instead when it matches the current top.
hints: Process characters left to right and keep the result so far; the only thing that can cancel is the most recently kept character.
A stack's top is exactly that most-recent character, so compare each new character against it.
If the incoming character equals the stack top, pop it (the pair vanishes); otherwise push it. Join the stack at the end.

edge_inputs.description: cascading removal after the first collapse
inner pair removed leaving the outside
triple of the same letter leaves one

stress.description: long strings over a tiny alphabet so collapses are frequent
